name: review-system
when_to_use: When you need to review a system specification before it is committed.
When the TUI should synchronously inspect the system spec for missing or weak requirements.

steps.description: Review the current requirements and approach context first.
steps.details: Use the upstream context to judge whether the system spec is complete enough to trust.

steps.description: Run `powdrr-lift evaluate-system-specification --work-item-name <work-item-name>` on the filled template.
steps.details: Treat any validation failure as a blocking issue before moving on to subjective review.

steps.description: Check the requirements and approach for missing context, weak statements, or inconsistent supersedence.
steps.details: Look for vague requirements, gaps between requirements and approach, and stale supersedence references.

steps.description: Report findings and repeat the review until the system spec validates cleanly.
steps.details: Keep feedback specific to the individual issue so the user can make the smallest possible fix.
